title: [InstCombine] Avoid simplifying bitcast of undef to a zeroinitializer vector

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-darwin10.0.0"

define i16 @bitcast_select_undef_to_vector(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 undef, i32 42
  %vec = bitcast i32 %sel to <2 x i16>
  %elem = extractelement <2 x i16> %vec, i64 0
  ret i16 %elem
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-darwin10.0.0"

define i16 @bitcast_select_undef_to_vector(i1 %cond) {
entry:
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i32 undef, i32 42
  %vec = bitcast i32 %sel to <2 x i16>
  %elem = extractelement <2 x i16> %vec, i64 0
  ret i16 %elem
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions phi with undef but not select instruction with undef operand. This tests undef propagation through select rather than phi node.

  %vec = bitcast i32 %sel to <2 x i16>

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-darwin10.0.0"

define i16 @bitcast_trunc_undef_to_vector() {
entry:
  %trunc = trunc i64 undef to i32
  %vec = bitcast i32 %trunc to <2 x i16>
  %elem = extractelement <2 x i16> %vec, i64 0
  ret i16 %elem
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-darwin10.0.0"

define i16 @bitcast_trunc_undef_to_vector() {
entry:
  %trunc = trunc i64 undef to i32
  %vec = bitcast i32 %trunc to <2 x i16>
  %elem = extractelement <2 x i16> %vec, i64 0
  ret i16 %elem
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't cover type conversion chains (trunc/zext/sext) that produce undef before the bitcast to vector.

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-darwin10.0.0"

define i8 @bitcast_undef_to_wide_vector() {
entry:
  %vec = bitcast i64 undef to <8 x i8>
  %elem = extractelement <8 x i8> %vec, i64 3
  ret i8 %elem
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64"
target triple = "x86_64-apple-darwin10.0.0"

define i8 @bitcast_undef_to_wide_vector() {
entry:
  %vec = bitcast i64 undef to <8 x i8>
  %elem = extractelement <8 x i8> %vec, i64 3
  ret i8 %elem
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary examples use <2 x i16> vectors from i32, this tests wider vectors (<8 x i8> from i64) with different element counts and types.
